search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae seborrheic dermatitis issue 2 dandruff history features red flags
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic shampoo, antifungal, steroid, dose, duration, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Dandruff has overlapping causes and requires clinical assessment.
No product or treatment instruction was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with recurrent scalp flaking or clinician-suspected seborrheic dermatitis.
setting_applicability: UAE telehealth, primary, and dermatology follow-up.
UAE_applicability: The primary source is UAE DHA guidance; direct examination and prescribing remain clinician-dependent.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The UAE DHA seborrheic-dermatitis guideline supports documenting scalp scale, itch, distribution, duration, recurrence, triggers, impact, morphology, and change when dandruff is clinically considered within that spectrum.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The red-flag section supports recording severe, widespread, infected, immunocompromised, or uncertain presentations only when assessed, plus clinician decisions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

unresolved_source_gaps: Scalp and other sites, morphology, itch, oiliness, triggers, prior products, response and adverse effects, differential, diagnosis, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
